clinical_history: ECG from a 74 year old woman with progressive increase in anginal-type chest discomfort. Which of the following statements is NOT correct?
diagnosis: This ECG is within normal limits. Yet the patient had severe underlying coronary artery disease, was status post coronary artery bypass graft surgery with recurrent angina. A normal (or relatively normal) resting ECG does not exclude severe, multivessel coronary disease. Further, even patients with advanced coronary disease can have false negative exercise ECGs.
